issue_description:
This is now my sixth report about the constantly overflowing / left-wide-open dumpster in the rear of this property, at the end of Edmunds St (a street with multiple rodent sightings on a daily basis). Just one week since my last photo and report (https://seeclickfix.com/web_portal/WacEfzPx7m29dubrPfXEEwCV/issues/19840464), and here we are again.

I have actively seen rats on my property on the same block as this dumpster every night last week (caught on camera), and while I personally follow Cambridge's rodent-control guidance, nothing I do will help while our neighbor here *actively* feeds rats with their huge open dumpster on a nightly basis right down the street.

(Also, aside from the rodents, this is just gross; this overflowing, smelly pile of trash is always visible directly next to the bike trail and isn't representing Cambridge in a good light at all).

It seems like repeated finger-wags and citations are not the solution to getting this owner to get a properly sized or seale...

I am writing to report a serious public health concern at the Landmark Kendall Square Cinema located at 355 Binney St, Cambridge, MA 02139.

On my recent visit, I personally witnessed a rat running through the theatre and later heard one squeaking during the film. This establishment serves food to patrons, which raises significant health and sanitation concerns given the clear evidence of a rodent infestation.

When I brought this issue to the attention of the only staff member working at the time, they confirmed that the cinema is already aware of the infestation. Based on their response and the fact that many recent online reviews also reference the same issue, I am deeply concerned that no corrective action is being taken.

I respectfully request that the Health Department investigate this matter as soon as possible to ensure the safety and well-being of the public.

Thank you for your attention to this issue.
